title: disocactus quezaltecus
intro: Disocactus quezaltecus, also known as the Aztec rainbow cactus, is a species of flowering cactus native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are used to treat a variety of ailments.
